summary: Completed S7 PR-08 OPML import/export cutover: backend import/export endpoints with feed-identity normalization and validation, BFF proxy routes, subscriptions UI import modal + export action, and acceptance-driven test coverage.
how_to_test: cd python && DATABASE_URL=postgresql+psycopg://postgres:postgres@localhost:54322/nexus_test uv run pytest -q tests/test_podcasts.py::TestPodcastOpmlImportExport && cd ../apps/web && npm test -- "src/app/api/podcasts/podcasts-routes.test.ts" "src/app/(authenticated)/podcasts/podcasts-flows.test.tsx" && cd ../python && DATABASE_URL=postgresql+psycopg://postgres:postgres@localhost:54322/nexus_test uv run pytest -q tests/test_podcasts.py
risks: OPML import executes provider lookup and transactional writes per outline; throughput is bounded by the 200-outline cap but may need batching if limits grow.
Export emits the required core OPML fields (xmlUrl/text/htmlUrl) but omits optional app-specific metadata some importers may support.
Provider identity collisions across feed URLs are handled defensively but remain a rare edge case that may need reconciliation tooling if seen in production.
